howItWorks.intro: This pet insurance cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Is pet insurance worth it?
faq.answer: If your pet has a major illness or injury ($3,000-$10,000+), insurance pays for itself. About 1 in 3 pets needs emergency care each year. Insurance prevents financial-based medical decisions.

faq.question: When should I enroll?
faq.answer: As early as possible — premiums are lowest for young pets and pre-existing conditions are excluded. Enrolling at adoption is ideal.

faq.question: What is not covered?
faq.answer: Pre-existing conditions (anything diagnosed before enrollment), cosmetic procedures, breeding costs, and preventive care (unless you buy a wellness add-on).

faq.question: How does reimbursement work?
faq.answer: You pay the vet, submit a claim, and get reimbursed 70-90% after deductible. Some plans now offer direct vet payment.
